Name.description: The name of the accepted data grant.

SenderPrincipal.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the data grant sender.

ReceiverPrincipal.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the data grant receiver.

Description.description: The description of the accepted data grant.

AcceptanceState.description: The acceptance state of the data grant.

AcceptedAt.description: The timestamp of when the data grant was accepted.

EndsAt.description: The timestamp of when access to the associated data set ends.

GrantDistributionScope.description: The distribution scope for the data grant.

DataSetId.description: The ID of the data set associated to the data grant.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the accepted data grant.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the data grant was created.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the data grant was last updated.

SenderPrincipal.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the data grant sender.

ReceiverPrincipal.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the data grant receiver.

AcceptanceState.description: The acceptance state of the data grant.

AcceptedAt.description: The timestamp of when the data grant was accepted.

EndsAt.description: The timestamp of when access to the associated data set ends.

GrantDistributionScope.description: The distribution scope for the data grant.

DataSetId.description: The ID of the data set associated to the data grant.

SourceDataSetId.description: The ID of the data set used to create the data grant.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the data grant.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the data grant was created.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the data grant was last updated.

Tags.description: The tags associated to the data grant. A tag is a key-value pair.

AssetType.description: The type of asset that is added to a data set.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the data set was created, in ISO 8601 format.

Id.description: The unique identifier for the data set.

Origin.description: A property that defines the data set as OWNED by the account (for providers) or ENTITLED to the account (for subscribers).

OriginDetails.description: If the origin of this data set is ENTITLED, includes the details for the product on AWS Marketplace.

SourceId.description: The data set ID of the owned data set corresponding to the entitled data set being viewed. This parameter is returned when a data set owner is viewing the entitled copy of its owned data set.

UpdatedAt.description: The date and time that the data set was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the event action was created, in ISO 8601 format.

Id.description: The unique identifier for the event action.

UpdatedAt.description: The date and time that the event action was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the job was created, in ISO 8601 format.

UpdatedAt.description: The date and time that the job was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the revision was created, in ISO 8601 format.

DataSetId.description: The unique identifier for the data set associated with the data set revision.

Finalized.description: To publish a revision to a data set in a product, the revision must first be finalized. Finalizing a revision tells AWS Data Exchange that your changes to the assets in the revision are complete. After it's in this read-only state, you can publish the revision to your products. Finalized revisions can be published through the AWS Data Exchange console or the AWS Marketplace Catalog API, using the StartChangeSet AWS Marketplace Catalog API action. When using the API, revisions are uniquely identified by their ARN.

SourceId.description: The revision ID of the owned revision corresponding to the entitled revision being viewed. This parameter is returned when a revision owner is viewing the entitled copy of its owned revision.

UpdatedAt.description: The date and time that the revision was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

RevocationComment.description: A required comment to inform subscribers of the reason their access to the revision was revoked.

Revoked.description: A status indicating that subscribers' access to the revision was revoked.

RevokedAt.description: The date and time that the revision was revoked, in ISO 8601 format.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the revision was created, in ISO 8601 format.

DataSetId.description: The unique identifier for the data set associated with the data set revision.

Finalized.description: To publish a revision to a data set in a product, the revision must first be finalized. Finalizing a revision tells AWS Data Exchange that changes to the assets in the revision are complete. After it's in this read-only state, you can publish the revision to your products. Finalized revisions can be published through the AWS Data Exchange console or the AWS Marketplace Catalog API, using the StartChangeSet AWS Marketplace Catalog API action. When using the API, revisions are uniquely identified by their ARN.

SourceId.description: The revision ID of the owned revision corresponding to the entitled revision being viewed. This parameter is returned when a revision owner is viewing the entitled copy of its owned revision.

UpdatedAt.description: The date and time that the revision was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

RevocationComment.description: A required comment to inform subscribers of the reason their access to the revision was revoked.

Revoked.description: A status indicating that subscribers' access to the revision was revoked.

RevokedAt.description: The date and time that the revision was revoked, in ISO 8601 format.

Body.description: The response body from the underlying API tracked by the API asset.

ResponseHeaders.description: The response headers from the underlying API tracked by the API asset.

AssetType.description: The type of asset that is added to a data set.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the asset was created, in ISO 8601 format.

DataSetId.description: The unique identifier for the data set associated with this asset.

Name.description: The name of the asset. When importing from Amazon S3, the Amazon S3 object key is used as the asset name. When exporting to Amazon S3, the asset name is used as default target Amazon S3 object key. When importing from Amazon API Gateway API, the API name is used as the asset name. When importing from Amazon Redshift, the datashare name is used as the asset name. When importing from AWS Lake Formation, the static values of "Database(s) included in the LF-tag policy"- or "Table(s) included in LF-tag policy" are used as the asset name.

RevisionId.description: The unique identifier for the revision associated with this asset.

SourceId.description: The asset ID of the owned asset corresponding to the entitled asset being viewed. This parameter is returned when an asset owner is viewing the entitled copy of its owned asset.

UpdatedAt.description: The date and time that the asset was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

AssetType.description: The type of asset that is added to a data set.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the data set was created, in ISO 8601 format.

Id.description: The unique identifier for the data set.

Origin.description: A property that defines the data set as OWNED by the account (for providers) or ENTITLED to the account (for subscribers).

UpdatedAt.description: The date and time that the data set was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the event action was created, in ISO 8601 format.

Id.description: The unique identifier for the event action.

UpdatedAt.description: The date and time that the event action was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time that the revision was created, in ISO 8601 format.

DataSetId.description: The unique identifier for the data set associated with the data set revision.
